conflict: the second naming forces the Mera-Kondo connection into public view; Venn's family integrity is threatened
consequence: the naming becomes a site of genuine conflict; Deshi's unexpected engagement overrides both Suna's refusal and Venn's resistance; Mava's tears make the connection feel real to witnesses
must_land_beats: Fasa calls the second naming and asks the Meras and Kondos to stand together
Suna refuses; Venn is insulted from the opposite direction
Deshi steps forward and addresses Mava directly — the most traditional figure becomes the most open
Mava weeps; the dialect word is confirmed as untraceable to any shared source
Venn tries to reassert control but the emotional evidence has already shifted the room
pressure_source: the second naming forces the Mera-Kondo connection into public view; Venn's family integrity is threatened
state_shift: the naming becomes a site of genuine conflict; Deshi's unexpected engagement overrides both Suna's refusal and Venn's resistance; Mava's tears make the connection feel real to witnesses
